principle: Difficulty is set by clinical-reasoning complexity. Any stem sentence that does not hook a distractor or steer the answer is padding and should be removed.

prunable_sentences: She has not eaten since lunch and her last menstrual period is not applicable.
She has no known drug allergies.
verdicts.rationale: LMP is not applicable to a 7-year-old and fasting status is not referenced by any option/rationale; allergy status is not hooked by any distractor (no allergy-related options).

prunable_sentences: (this hooks the discharge-home and urinalysis-only distractors, since neonatal age alone mandates full septic workup regardless of source)
He is fully immunised for age (none yet due).
verdicts.rationale: Parenthetical is a leaked author meta-note that should never appear in a stem. Immunisation status (none due yet at 24 days) provides no diagnostic information and does not hook any distractor.

prunable_sentences: Her parents are present and have consented to investigations.
verdicts.rationale: Consent statement does not steer any option choice; no distractor discriminates on consent. The TB exposure, VP shunt, penicillin urticaria, focal seizures, and immunisation status all hook specific options (D, vancomycin coverage, cephalosporin safety, LP deferral, Hib coverage respectively).

prunable_sentences: There is no family history of inflammatory bowel disease.
verdicts.rationale: IBD family history is not on the differential for a 28-week neonate with NEC features and no option discriminates on IBD. Caffeine, recent transfusion absence, mottling, bilious aspirate, bloody stool all hook NEC vs alternatives appropriately.

prunable_sentences: There is a family history of coeliac disease but no thyroid disease.
Her allergies are nil known.
verdicts.rationale: Coeliac/thyroid family history is type-1-DM-associated background but does not change next acute step or hook any distractor. Allergy status is not referenced by any option/rationale.

prunable_sentences: He has had no recent travel.
He weighs 19 kg, has no known drug allergies, and his recent levetiracetam level was therapeutic.
verdicts.rationale: Recent travel is irrelevant to status epilepticus and not referenced by any option. Weight is already given earlier in stem; therapeutic levetiracetam level does not change second-line choice (option B still loads levetiracetam per APLS regardless of pre-existing level in acute status); allergy status is not hooked by any option.

prunable_sentences: He has had no recent travel outside the region and no household pets or insect bites.
He has no known drug allergies.
His weight and growth are on the 25th centile.
verdicts.rationale: Travel/pets/insect bites would only matter for a zoonotic or vector-borne differential that is not represented in any option. Allergy status is not hooked by any option. Growth centile is unreferenced by any rationale.

prunable_sentences: He has no documented bleeding disorder and no known medical conditions.
verdicts.rationale: Option C explicitly says 'attribute to a presumed undiagnosed inherited bleeding disorder' - the rationale for ruling out C is that normal coagulation does not exclude NAI, not that a bleeding disorder is absent. The 'no documented bleeding disorder' statement therefore does not hook C; if anything it pre-empts the option. Removing it does not change difficulty.

prunable_sentences: She had a first-trimester combined risk screen and a normal 20-week morphology scan.
She is a non-smoker, BMI 31, allergic to penicillin (rash), influenza-vaccinated this pregnancy and has had her pertussis booster at 28 weeks.
verdicts.rationale: Combined screen/morphology scan are antenatal completeness padding that do not hook any SOMANZ-criteria-based option. Smoking, BMI, penicillin allergy, vaccinations are not referenced by any option rationale or SOMANZ criterion in this question.

prunable_sentences: (this hooks the immediate-Caesarean distractor, since prior preterm vaginal birth is not in itself an indication for operative delivery now)

She is on no regular medications, has no allergies, smoking 5 cigarettes per day (declined to cease), and lives 90 minutes from the nearest tertiary NICU (currently in that NICU, so transfer-out is not indicated).
verdicts.rationale: Both parenthetical author meta-notes are leaked review comments that must not appear in a stem. Without the meta-note about being in the NICU, the residency-on-no-meds/smoking/distance sentence loses its anchor for the option-E ex-utero-transfer distractor; however that distractor is already invalidated by stem opening ('tertiary maternity service'). Smoking/allergies/medications are not referenced by any option.

prunable_sentences: Her three previous births were uncomplicated.
verdicts.rationale: Prior uncomplicated births do not hook any uterotonic-choice distractor and are not referenced in any option rationale. Coagulopathy/anticoagulants/prior PPH and admission Hb all hook plausible alternatives.

prunable_sentences: (this hooks the COCP and patch distractors, since migraine with aura is an absolute UKMEC 4 contraindication to combined hormonal contraception)
She had a Caesarean for breech with her first child and two subsequent uneventful vaginal births.
Her cervical screening test is up to date and negative.
verdicts.rationale: Author meta-comment leaked into stem. Past obstetric history (Caesarean, vaginal births) does not change UKMEC categories for the listed options. Cervical screening status is unreferenced by any contraception option.

prunable_sentences: There are no antenatal risk factors for placental insufficiency.
She is hydrated and her last paracetamol was 6 hours ago.
verdicts.rationale: Absence of placental-insufficiency risk does not hook any option (none of A-E rest on this). Hydration status and paracetamol timing are not referenced by any option rationale.

prunable_sentences: She has met all developmental milestones to date, is fully immunised to the current Australian schedule, and has no family history of epilepsy or sudden infant death.
The mother denies head trauma and no household members are on antiepileptic or antidepressant medications.

The team has applied high-flow oxygen at 8 L/min via Hudson mask, has charcoal-impregnated suction available at the bedside, has prepared a rapid-sequence induction trolley, and the anaesthetic registrar and PICU outreach team have been paged.
verdicts.rationale: Developmental milestones, immunisations, family history of epilepsy/SIDS are background colour that do not change the next pharmacological step. Head trauma absence and household drug access are not referenced by any option (no toxic/traumatic option exists). The team-readiness sentence (charcoal suction, RSI trolley) does not change the choice between levetiracetam and phenytoin/phenobarbitone/RSI.

prunable_sentences: He has a 22-gauge cannula in the right antecubital fossa and a second cannula being placed; his last meal was 4 hours ago.
There are no known drug allergies and parents have given verbal consent for further therapy.
verdicts.rationale: Cannula gauge/location and fasting status do not differentiate IV magnesium from IV salbutamol/aminophylline (all are IV). Allergy status and consent are not hooked by any option.

prunable_sentences: She had no preceding head injury, no toxic ingestion, and no prior diabetes diagnosis; her family history is notable only for type 2 diabetes in a paternal uncle.
verdicts.rationale: Head injury/ingestion/family history of T2DM do not change management of the established DKA-related cerebral oedema clinical picture; no option discriminates on prior diabetes or family history.

prunable_sentences: The on-call mental health team has been notified and is on the way.
The on-call clinical toxicologist has been contacted by phone.
verdicts.rationale: Mental-health notification and toxicology consultation are governance steps that do not change the NAC decision itself; no option discriminates on whether the consultation has occurred.

prunable_sentences: He was born at 39+4 weeks by uncomplicated vaginal delivery, with Apgars 9 and 9, and discharged from the maternity unit at 36 hours after a normal newborn examination.
There is no family history of congenital heart disease.
verdicts.rationale: Delivery details, Apgars, discharge timing, and family history of CHD are background that do not discriminate between the listed pharmacological options once the bedside echo has confirmed critical coarctation with closing duct.

prunable_sentences: He has had one apnoea requiring stimulation in the last hour.
He has no umbilical lines in situ.
He has had no surgical procedures and no recent transfusions.
He has not yet received probiotics.
verdicts.rationale: Apnoea episode does not change NEC management choice between the options. No-umbilical-lines, no-prior-surgery, no-prior-transfusion, no-probiotics are explicitly non-discriminating: no option turns on these factors (probiotic option A is ruled out by active NEC presence not by prior probiotic status).

prunable_sentences: There is no family history of hearing loss or congenital infection, no maternal HIV or syphilis, and rubella immunity was documented at booking.
verdicts.rationale: Once CMV is confirmed by PCR (per lead-in), absence of hearing-loss family history, maternal HIV/syphilis, and rubella immunity does not change the choice between valganciclovir, IV ganciclovir, observation, hyperimmune globulin, or audiology-only.

prunable_sentences: He weighs 3540 g and has no surgical contraindication.
Imaging is pending.
verdicts.rationale: Weight is not used to discriminate cooling method (servo cooling temperature is not weight-dependent in the options). 'No surgical contraindication' is not hooked by any option. Pending imaging does not discriminate between options once eligibility for cooling is established (option E about delaying for MRI is invalid regardless of whether imaging is pending or done).

prunable_sentences: There is no pneumothorax on transillumination or repeat CXR.
verdicts.rationale: Absence of pneumothorax does not differentiate iNO from HFOV/ECMO/sildenafil/PGE1 - no option proposes pneumothorax treatment. Surfactant given, milrinone, structurally normal heart, OI 22, ductal R-to-L all hook specific options.

prunable_sentences: There is no family history of consanguinity, neonatal seizures or sudden infant death.
Examination reveals no dysmorphic features, no rash, no neurocutaneous stigmata (no cafe-au-lait or hypopigmented macules under Wood's lamp), and a normal anterior fontanelle.
verdicts.rationale: Family history details and absence of dysmorphic/neurocutaneous features do not discriminate among diazepam, calcium, pyridoxine, phenobarbitone, or hypothermia. Pyridoxine option C exclusion rests on it being reserved for refractory cases, not on absence of family history. The HIE-criteria sentence already addresses cooling option E.

prunable_sentences: There is no antenatal evidence of infection.
He has not yet had a chest radiograph.
verdicts.rationale: Infection status is already addressed by the earlier sentence about CRP/WCC and no maternal fever. CXR status does not change choice between LISA, intubation, INSURE, nebulised surfactant, or postnatal steroid.

prunable_sentences: She has not previously had any cardiac investigations or family screening, she takes no over-the-counter or herbal medications, and her resting heart rate today is 58 with no postural drop.
There is no history of hearing impairment in the family.
verdicts.rationale: Prior absence of cardiac investigations does not change current management. OTC/herbal medications and postural drop are not referenced by any option. Hearing impairment (Jervell-Lange-Nielsen vs Romano-Ward distinction) is not discriminated by any of the options A-E.

prunable_sentences: She has had no rash and no subcutaneous nodules detected on careful palpation of extensor surfaces.
verdicts.rationale: Absence of rash and subcutaneous nodules (other major criteria) is not directly hooked by any of the five options about whether the diagnostic threshold is met; the discriminating points are polyarthritis major, minors (PR/CRP/ESR), and GAS evidence which all remain.

prunable_sentences: He has not had any significant illnesses and has been fully immunised.
Blood pressure is symmetric in both arms at 96/58 mmHg and femoral pulses are present and equal.
verdicts.rationale: Past medical/immunisation history does not discriminate among the syndromes listed. Symmetric BP and present femoral pulses rule out coarctation, but coarctation is not on the option list; none of the syndromes A-E proposes coarctation as the cardiac lesion.

prunable_sentences: He has no learning difficulties, no recent syncope, and no exertional chest pain.
verdicts.rationale: Absence of learning difficulties, syncope, and chest pain does not discriminate among the medical-therapy options A-E. Syncope/chest pain would prompt urgent intervention but no option proposes that based on these features.

prunable_sentences: He has had no seizures and has not lost previously acquired skills.
verdicts.rationale: Absence of seizures and regression does not discriminate among the five syndromes listed. Microarray-normal/metabolic-screen-normal is retained because option E rationale specifically references microarrays not detecting trinucleotide expansions, which is the key teaching point.

prunable_sentences: The consultant intensivist is en route from the ICU upstairs, the airway trolley with appropriately sized equipment is at the bedside, and 3% saline and mannitol are available in the resus bay.
verdicts.rationale: Availability of consultant, airway trolley, and osmotherapy agents does not discriminate among the options - 3% saline (option C) is correct regardless of whether equipment is mentioned as ready, and the LP/bicarbonate/dextrose options are wrong on clinical grounds not equipment availability.

prunable_sentences: Her past medical and developmental history is unremarkable and there is no family history of central or peripheral precocious puberty, McCune-Albright syndrome, neurofibromatosis or congenital adrenal hyperplasia.
The thyroid is not palpable, the abdomen is soft with no palpable masses and no organomegaly, and the neurological examination including fundoscopy and visual fields by confrontation is normal.
verdicts.rationale: Negative family history of named conditions and benign thyroid/abdominal/neurological exam findings do not discriminate between GnRH stimulation testing and the other options (pelvic MRI, 17-OHP, MRI brain, empirical leuprolide). The discriminating features are already captured: B3 breast (gonadarche), accelerated growth, advanced bone age, pubertal pelvic ultrasound.

prunable_sentences: There is no pretibial myxoedema and no thyroid acropachy.
verdicts.rationale: Pretibial myxoedema and thyroid acropachy absence does not discriminate among the medical management options (PTU, RAI, surgery, carbimazole+propranolol, Lugol's). The diagnosis of Graves is already firmly established by TRAb, suppressed TSH, elevated T4/T3.

prunable_sentences: His urinary specific gravity is 1.002 and the urine dipstick is otherwise negative.
verdicts.rationale: Specific gravity is redundant with the urine osmolality 110 already provided (both confirm dilute urine). Dipstick-negative is already addressed by the random glucose 4.8 ruling out DM (option D rationale). Sentence is informational filler.

prunable_sentences: She is taking no regular medications and has no known allergies.
verdicts.rationale: No medication/allergy history doesn't discriminate among empirical aciclovir, methylprednisolone, ceftriaxone+dex, EEG, or ribavirin. No drug allergy option discriminator exists.

prunable_sentences: He has no other identifiable contacts of concern, including childcare contacts, who have been screened by the public health unit.
There is no erythema nodosum and no phlyctenular conjunctivitis.
verdicts.rationale: Other contacts having been screened does not affect this child's management. Absence of erythema nodosum/phlyctenular conjunctivitis does not discriminate between options - active TB workup isn't on the option list and these features don't change LTBI treatment decision.

prunable_sentences: His mother is also concerned about her own immunity because she is 18 weeks pregnant with the family's fourth child, has no history of chickenpox or vaccination herself, and has just had a serology test sent that morning to clarify her immune status.
There is also a third sibling, a 12-year-old who has had clinically diagnosed varicella as a preschooler.
verdicts.rationale: Maternal pregnancy/immunity and 12-year-old sibling's history are context for an unasked question. The lead-in is specifically about this patient's intervention; no option turns on the mother's pregnancy or the older sibling's immunity status.

prunable_sentences: She has no penicillin or other drug allergy, no previous resistant organisms colonising her central line or stool surveillance, and her last echocardiogram performed 3 months ago for routine anthracycline monitoring showed normal LV function and a normal ejection fraction of 64%.
There is no history of mucositis bleeding, no recent abdominal pain and no diarrhoea.
The on-call oncology consultant and the paediatric intensive care registrar have been contacted and are aware.
verdicts.rationale: Echo function does not change empirical antibiotic regimen choice. No-mucositis/no-pain/no-diarrhoea does not discriminate among the antibiotic regimens listed (option C says add vanc if mucositis - but the discriminating point is the antipseudomonal beta-lactam + aminoglycoside which holds regardless). Allergy status not hooked by any option. Consultant awareness is governance.

prunable_sentences: First-degree relatives have no history of cystic fibrosis or congenital gut malformation.
verdicts.rationale: Family history of CF/congenital gut malformation does not discriminate among the imaging options - upper GI contrast is the test of choice for bilious vomiting regardless. Antenatal scan/polyhydramnios sentence hooks options about meconium ileus/duodenal atresia differential.

Every sentence earns its place: gestation/weight establishes NEC risk, feed history justifies trophic-to-60 advancement context, all clinical signs (girth, BP, lactate, CRP fall, platelet fall, WCC fall) are Bell-stage criteria that hook option D vs A (no antibiotics), B (continue feeds), C (urgent laparotomy), E (drain). Bluish flank discoloration distinguishes from non-ischaemic causes. Free-air absence on lateral decubitus directly hooks C and E options. Surgical consultation by phone is procedural anchor.

prunable_sentences: She has had no fevers, no overseas travel, no antibiotic exposure in the last 12 months, and is up to date with all routine immunisations.
verdicts.rationale: Travel/antibiotic/immunisation history does not discriminate among the diagnostic-pathway options. Family history of CD in sibling is retained because it supports the ESPGHAN no-biopsy consideration in option B's rationale, even though it is ultimately rejected.

prunable_sentences: Weight is 13 kg.
verdicts.rationale: Weight not used in any option dosing decision (all options give dexamethasone in mg/kg which doesn't differentiate, or fixed doses). Hib immunisation, no atopy, no FB hook the epiglottitis/asthma/FB differentials properly.

prunable_sentences: She is otherwise well, growing along the 50th centile and has normal ENT and dermatological examinations.
There is no rhinitis, no eczema and no GORD symptoms.
She has no allergy or family history requiring specialist input.
verdicts.rationale: Growth/ENT/dermatology exam, absence of rhinitis/eczema/GORD, and absence of family allergy history do not discriminate among the step-up options (montelukast, ICS-LABA, MART, biologic, LAMA). The discriminating features are poor control on low-dose ICS with confirmed adherence/technique.

prunable_sentences: He weighs 4.8 kg.
verdicts.rationale: Weight not referenced by any option dosing decision; the discriminator is age <6 months for azithromycin preference and admission requirement.

prunable_sentences: There is no school attendance issue and the family lives 15 minutes from the nearest ED.
The school has a copy of the previous action plan but it is now 18 months old.
verdicts.rationale: Distance to ED and school's outdated action plan are not referenced by any yellow-zone advice option. The discriminating features (history of late escalation, oral pred each time) hook the early-intensification advice.

prunable_sentences: She is fully immunised including annual influenza and is up to date with the seasonal RSV programme.
Her weight is 38 kg.
verdicts.rationale: Influenza/RSV immunisation status does not discriminate among antibiotic regimens. Weight not used in any option dosing decision (all mg/kg). Modulator therapy, baseline FEV1, sputum culture, creatinine all earn their place.

prunable_sentences: His past medical history is unremarkable, he takes no regular medications, he has no known allergies and his immunisations are up to date.
There is no family history of allergic disease, asthma, or cardiac disorders.
He had been camping with his family at a regional park and was helping clear food scraps from the picnic table when stung on the right forearm.
He has no relevant drug allergies, no recent fasting, and a full set of bloods including tryptase has been sent.
verdicts.rationale: PMH/medications/immunisations/family history do not discriminate among adrenaline regimens. Camping context (sting source) is background; the cause is established. Repeat drug-allergy mention is redundant; tryptase/bloods sent does not change next intervention choice.

prunable_sentences: The child has no congenital heart disease.
She has no known drug allergies and her immunisations including 13-valent pneumococcal and influenza are up to date.
There is no chronic medical condition, no recent travel, no immunocompromise and no contact with an unwell sibling.
verdicts.rationale: Congenital heart disease absence does not discriminate among inotropes for cold septic shock. Immunisations and allergy not referenced by any option. Travel/immunocompromise/sibling-contact do not discriminate among the inotrope choice. Bedside echo (hyperdynamic underfilled LV) IS hooked by option C noradrenaline (warm vs cold shock) so retained.

prunable_sentences: There is no family history of bleeding disorder, no preceding viral illness, and no recent immunisation in the last 7 days.
verdicts.rationale: Coagulation screen, FBC, LFTs already normal; family history of bleeding disorder/viral illness/recent immunisation does not change response when the discriminator is sentinel bruising in a pre-mobile infant. Option A (coag-only) is wrong on principle, not on family history.

prunable_sentences: The pool was uncovered and the temperature ambient.
There was no chlorine exposure or chemical contamination of the water.
She had no known medical conditions, no medications, no allergies, and her immunisations are up to date.
There is no family history of arrhythmia, sudden cardiac death, long QT syndrome, or epilepsy that might have preceded the submersion.
Her father witnessed the entire event from the kitchen window and estimates the maximum time submerged at 5 minutes.
verdicts.rationale: Pool cover and chemical contamination do not discriminate (option C about antibiotics is wrong on principle for any clean-water drowning - the rationale states antibiotics are for evidence of infection or specific organisms regardless). PMH/family arrhythmia history does not change post-arrest care choices. Father's witness account is duplicated with the earlier estimate.

prunable_sentences: She has no history of self-harm, no current psychiatric medications, no chronic medical illness and no regular paracetamol or hepatic enzyme-inducing medications.
She lives at home with her parents, attends Year 10 and has been struggling academically and socially over the past 2 months.
Her mother brought her in immediately on finding the empty packet and a written note in her bedroom.
The CAMHS on-call clinician has been contacted, the empty Panadol packet has been retained for documentation, and the toxicology registrar has reviewed the bloods and the timing of the level.
verdicts.rationale: Repeat statement of no enzyme-inducing drugs (already covered earlier in stem). Social/educational history does not discriminate among the toxicological management options. Mother-brought-in narrative and CAMHS/toxicology consult details do not change the NAC decision.

prunable_sentences: There is no fluorescein uptake on the left cornea on bedside slit-lamp by the ED registrar, no preauricular lymphadenopathy, and his capillary refill is 2 seconds.
He has been started on IV maintenance fluids and a swab from a deroofed vesicle has been couriered to the lab for HSV PCR.
verdicts.rationale: First sentence duplicates the earlier 'no ocular involvement on fluorescein staining'; preauricular lymphadenopathy/CRT are not referenced by any option. The IV fluids/swab sentence does not change choice of treatment - option D's correctness rests on the diagnosis and need for IV aciclovir, not on prior swab handling.

prunable_sentences: There is no maternal history of opioid or recreational drug use in pregnancy.
Calorie intake by parental report is approximately 130 mL/kg/day of standard infant formula.
The surgical registrar has been contacted and an IV cannula sited; nasogastric tube is on standby for decompression while imaging is reviewed.
verdicts.rationale: Maternal opioid use (neonatal abstinence-related delayed meconium) is not represented in the option list. Caloric intake does not differentiate among diagnoses. The surgical-contact/IV cannula sentence is procedural and does not discriminate among diagnoses.

prunable_sentences: His mother has separately disclosed that she also suspects ADHD in herself and is interested in being assessed after her son's care is settled.
He takes no regular medications and has no known drug allergies.
verdicts.rationale: Mother's possible ADHD does not discriminate among the options for the patient's management. Allergies/medications absence is unreferenced by any option (no drug-interaction option exists).

prunable_sentences: She has had no inpatient medical or psychiatric admissions previously.
She has supportive friendships at school but has withdrawn from her netball team over the past 2 months.
She is not on any regular medications.
Her mother has a history of depression treated with sertraline some years ago.
verdicts.rationale: Prior admissions, school friendships/withdrawal, current medications, and maternal depression treatment do not discriminate among the management options. The discriminating features (passive ideation no plan/intent, willing for mother involvement, brother bipolar) are retained.

prunable_sentences: She had a normal birth and early development, sat at 6 months, walked at 13 months, achieved menarche at 12, and was previously academically high-performing.
She has not previously engaged with any mental health service.
Her parents are both present at the bedside and engaged in care.
verdicts.rationale: Developmental milestones and early academic performance do not discriminate among the management options. Prior mental-health service contact and parental presence are not referenced by any option rationale; the discriminator is medical instability requiring admission.

prunable_sentences: The 36-week growth scan showed an appropriately grown foetus on the 55th centile with normal Dopplers.
Maternal observations are stable: BP 118/72, HR 88, T 36.9, SpO2 99% on room air.
verdicts.rationale: Normal growth/Dopplers and normal maternal observations do not discriminate among the management options - the discriminator is the pathological CTG and the resuscitation + foetal scalp lactate response.

prunable_sentences: She is currently positioned semi-recumbent with a left lateral wedge in place.
verdicts.rationale: Positioning is appropriate but does not discriminate among vasopressor options (ephedrine vs metaraminol vs phenylephrine vs adrenaline vs atropine). Other features (LAST-screening symptoms absence, no cord prolapse, normal CTG features prior) hook appropriate distractors.

prunable_sentences: She is otherwise well, BMI 28, non-smoker, no cocaine or amphetamine use, no antihypertensive use, and no history of chronic hypertension or pre-eclampsia in previous pregnancies.
verdicts.rationale: BMI/smoking/antihypertensive history/hypertension history do not discriminate among the diagnoses. Cocaine/amphetamine absence is partially relevant to option B (abruption) but option B is already addressed by 'no abdominal pain' and 'no abdominal trauma' earlier in the stem.

prunable_sentences: She is otherwise well, has not received antenatal corticosteroids in this pregnancy, has no asthma, no contraindications to any single antihypertensive agent, and the neonatal team has been notified for likely preterm delivery.
verdicts.rationale: Asthma absence would only matter if a beta-blocker contraindication were in question, but the stem explicitly states no contraindications - making the whole sentence circular. Antenatal corticosteroid status, neonatal team notification do not discriminate among the antihypertensive choices.

prunable_sentences: Her three previous births were unassisted vaginal deliveries at term with no complications.
Her booking BMI was 26, she has no cardiac, respiratory or thromboembolic history, no allergies, and was on no regular medications.
Her booking bloods including FBC, group and screen, and antibody screen were unremarkable, and her 36-week growth scan showed a normally grown foetus.
verdicts.rationale: Previous deliveries, BMI, thromboembolic/allergy/medication history, and booking bloods/growth scan do not discriminate among the diagnoses (AFE/LAST/eclampsia/PE/ACS). The discriminating features (sudden post-placental collapse, hypoxia, DIC oozing from puncture sites, no hypertension/proteinuria, only 5 mL lignocaine) all earn their place.

prunable_sentences: She has not had a seizure but has a mild bifrontal headache and no visual scotomata.
verdicts.rationale: Headache/seizure/scotomata absence are pre-eclampsia features but the option list does not distinguish PE vs HELLP explicitly (HELLP is on PE spectrum and the discriminator is the biochemistry profile, which is fully captured). Neuro symptom mention does not change diagnosis selection.

prunable_sentences: She is referred to maternal-foetal medicine to discuss treatment options pending foetal status, and asks specifically whether the medication chosen now should change if foetal infection is later confirmed on amniotic fluid PCR.

The maternal-foetal medicine consultant also outlines the typical vertical transmission risk by trimester and explains why the timing of investigations matters, before turning to the choice of initial pharmacotherapy.
verdicts.rationale: The patient-question framing and the consultant counselling narrative are scene-setting; option A's correctness depends on the maternal/foetal infection status already established in the rest of the stem, not on whether MFM has been involved in the conversation.

prunable_sentences: Her neurologist has reviewed her recent MRI and EEG which remain stable, and confirms she has reliable epilepsy without evidence of progressive structural lesion.
She does not drive a heavy vehicle for work and has well-controlled mood with no current depressive symptoms.

She also asks whether to expect a dose adjustment in pregnancy and what to do about breastfeeding postpartum, and her partner asks whether she should stop driving during the cross-titration period as a safety precaution.
verdicts.rationale: Stable MRI/EEG, occupational driving, partner's questions about cross-titration driving, and breastfeeding postpartum are not discriminating among the five recommendation packages (each option specifies AED choice + folate dose + monitoring, none turn on imaging stability or driving).

prunable_sentences: She takes her levothyroxine on an empty stomach 30 minutes before breakfast and does not take iron or calcium within four hours of her dose.
She is anti-TPO positive.
verdicts.rationale: Levothyroxine timing/iron-calcium avoidance is correct practice but does not discriminate among the dose-adjustment + monitoring options. Anti-TPO positivity supports the autoimmune aetiology but is already established by 'longstanding primary autoimmune hypothyroidism' in the opening sentence.

prunable_sentences: She had a similar mild episode 6 weeks ago that lasted 2 hours.
Her appendix has not been removed.
verdicts.rationale: A single prior episode already documented (3 months ago) sufficiently hooks the intermittent torsion pattern; an extra second prior episode adds no discriminatory information. Appendix presence does not discriminate among the diagnoses (appendicitis option E is rejected on time-course/USS findings, not on appendix presence).

prunable_sentences: She had mild gestational hypertension in the third trimester that resolved within 2 weeks postpartum, and her blood pressure today is 118/74 mmHg with no proteinuria.
Her partner is not pursuing vasectomy in the near term.
She has no contraindications to any specific contraceptive method on history.
verdicts.rationale: Resolved gestational hypertension with normalised BP does not change option choice (the relevant exclusions for COC are already covered by breastfeeding status, and option A's rationale rests on breastfeeding postpartum window). Partner vasectomy and 'no contraindications' are scene-setting.

prunable_sentences: She has trialled citalopram 20 mg daily for 4 months with minimal symptomatic benefit and is now seeking menopausal hormone therapy.
verdicts.rationale: SSRI trial failure is background to justify MHT use but does not discriminate among the MHT regimen options. The discriminators (intact uterus, prior VTE, postmenopausal, no-bleed preference) all earn their place.

prunable_sentences: Her treating breast oncologist is aware she is exploring further treatment options for her genitourinary symptoms and is supportive of considering local therapies after shared decision-making.
She has no other oestrogen-dependent malignancy and no undiagnosed bleeding.
verdicts.rationale: Oncologist awareness/support is process narrative; the correct answer (option C) describes shared decision-making with the oncologist as part of the management. Absence of other oestrogen-dependent malignancy and bleeding does not differentiate the local-oestrogen vs laser vs ospemifene options.

prunable_sentences: She has no other rashes, no oral lichen planus, no eye symptoms, no skin lesions elsewhere, and no urinary or bowel symptoms.
Her long-term partner has no penile lesions or symptoms.
She does not smoke.
She wonders whether she still needs ongoing specialist follow-up after 6 years of stable disease, or whether she could now safely self-manage with intermittent steroid use only as needed.
verdicts.rationale: Other dermatological/mucosal lichen sites, partner symptoms, smoking, and patient's question about follow-up cessation do not discriminate among the management options (biopsy-first vs increase-steroid vs add-tacrolimus vs wide-excision vs discharge). The discriminators (persistent fissured lesion despite optimised steroid, longstanding LS) are retained.

prunable_sentences: She received enoxaparin 40 mg subcutaneously daily for 10 days postpartum because of her BMI; an emergency caesarean was not performed.
verdicts.rationale: Postpartum enoxaparin completion does not discriminate among contraceptive options at 5 weeks postpartum (the VTE risk consideration is the COC vs progestogen choice, and absence of personal/family VTE history is already established by the no-VTE sentence). Implant remains best regardless of LMWH history.

prunable_sentences: She has tried sleep hygiene, increased exercise, and a referral to a perinatal psychologist with two sessions completed but no improvement in symptoms over the past fortnight.
verdicts.rationale: The non-pharmacological trial failure justifies pharmacotherapy in general but does not discriminate among the five SSRI/SNRI/lithium options. The discriminating feature is choice of agent compatible with breastfeeding.

prunable_sentences: She made an apparent physical recovery and was discharged on day 6 postpartum on iron supplementation.
verdicts.rationale: Discharge day and iron supplementation history do not discriminate among the workup strategies; the discriminator is the panhypopituitarism picture requiring full axis screen + MRI.

prunable_sentences: She mentions feeling low and tearful since day 3 postpartum and has not yet started any antidepressant.
She specifically asks today whether she still needs her medications.
verdicts.rationale: Depressive symptoms hint at relevance to methyldopa (depression risk) but option B rationale rejects methyldopa for postpartum routine reasons, not specifically for this patient's mood. Patient's question is conversational framing.

Every sentence earns its place: CS for breech, retained cotyledon (Sheehan-spectrum risk), no PPH but estimated blood loss, no antenatal breast enlargement, tubular widely spaced asymmetric breasts (insufficient glandular tissue), normal IBCLC review (rules out latch/suck), no breast surgery/piercings (rules out post-surgical), all directly hook the prolactin + pituitary axis option D vs tongue-tie/FBC/ferritin/ultrasound/domperidone alternatives.

prunable_sentences: , has no significant medical history, has no history of gallstones or biliary disease, drinks 4 standard drinks per week, and is on no regular medications other than the etonogestrel implant
verdicts.rationale: PMH/gallstone/alcohol/implant portion of the long admission sentence does not discriminate among options once gallbladder USS findings and PID workup are reported. Beta-hCG portion is retained because it hooks option D (ectopic exclusion).

prunable_sentences: Her hepatitis B and hepatitis C serology are negative, syphilis serology is negative, and the 36-week GBS swab is negative.
verdicts.rationale: Negative Hep B/C/syphilis/GBS do not discriminate among the HIV-specific delivery options (mode of delivery, IV ZDV, neonatal PEP, breastfeeding). The discriminators (suppressed viral load throughout T3, no obstetric contraindication) are retained.

prunable_sentences: She is asking what medications she should take, how long she needs to take them for, when she will be re-tested for HIV, and what side effects she should monitor for.
verdicts.rationale: Patient's question is framing for the lead-in; does not discriminate among the PEP regimen options. Discriminating features (high-risk exposure within 72h, source not on effective treatment, suitable renal function, no allergies) are retained.

prunable_sentences: She has had her three-dose HPV vaccine course as a teenager but does not know her hepatitis B vaccination status and does not recall having received the catch-up adolescent dose.
verdicts.rationale: HPV/HBV vaccination uncertainty does not discriminate among the management options - option E correctly bundles HBV vaccination regardless. Police-reporting indecision is retained because it hooks option C's incorrect linkage of forensic exam to police reporting.

prunable_sentences: Her last menstrual period was 35 days ago and she normally has a 28-day cycle, but she has had irregular cycles in the past.
She has had one sexual partner over the past year, uses no contraception and has had no recent abnormal vaginal bleeding or discharge.
verdicts.rationale: LMP/cycle details and sexual history are scene-setting for ectopic-pregnancy differential but option B is already definitively excluded by beta-hCG <5 IU/L in the stem. The discriminator for torsion is the Doppler finding + adnexal fullness + acute onset, which are retained.

Every sentence earns its place: low-risk cFTS (hooks NIPT distractor), isolated EIF with normal cardiac views (hooks foetal echo distractor), no other soft markers (hooks repeat-scan distractor), no FH of CHD (reinforces no echo indication), normal placenta/AFI/biometry rule out other markers.

Every sentence anchors a methotrexate eligibility criterion: prior reproductive history (no prior ectopic/IUD/tubal surgery hooks distractors about risk factors), stable haemodynamics, no peritonism, sonographic findings (size, yolk sac, no cardiac activity, no haemoperitoneum) all hook the criteria options A-E. Distance/transport and methotrexate-contraindication absence hook option E.

prunable_sentences: She has no significant past medical or surgical history, no risk factors for ectopic pregnancy (no prior pelvic infection, IUD, tubal surgery or assisted reproductive technology), and no contraindications to methotrexate.
verdicts.rationale: Absence of ectopic risk factors and methotrexate contraindications do not discriminate among the options - the central discriminator is hCG below discriminatory zone with PUL. Methotrexate option A is wrong because of PUL, not because of contraindications.

prunable_sentences: She has not been on any thyroid medication.
verdicts.rationale: Absence of thyroid medication is informational but does not discriminate among the GTD management options. Hyperthyroidism via beta-hCG hooks the diagnosis but the options are all about management approach.

prunable_sentences: She takes only luteal-phase progesterone pessaries 200 mg twice daily as part of her IVF protocol and a pregnancy multivitamin; she has no other medications and no drug allergies.
verdicts.rationale: Progesterone pessaries and multivitamin/allergies do not discriminate among the heterotopic-pregnancy management options. The discriminators (prior bilateral salpingectomies, IVF, viable IUP + adnexal mass + free fluid + worsening pain) all earn their place.

prunable_sentences: The CTG had been normal throughout and Apgar scores were 9 and 9.

Her booking BMI was 24, she has no medical comorbidities, GBS swab at 36 weeks was negative, and the antenatal course was uncomplicated apart from a borderline GTT that was normal on dietary management.
verdicts.rationale: Normal CTG/Apgars and antenatal/GBS/BMI history do not discriminate among the OASI classification/repair options. The discriminators (60% EAS thickness, intact IAS, intact rectal mucosa) are retained.

prunable_sentences: She is a non-smoker and took aspirin 100 mg from 12 weeks.
verdicts.rationale: Smoking status and aspirin prophylaxis (history) do not discriminate among the management options. The diagnosis of PE is already established by BP, PCR, gestation; the discriminator is gestation >=37 weeks plus severity features.
After review, no sentence can be cleanly removed without breaking discriminator coverage: BMI/comorbidity portion is in a sentence with GBS-negative status which hooks option B distinction.

prunable_sentences: Her booking BMI was 27, she has no medical comorbidities, the GBS swab at 36 weeks was negative and her 36-week scan showed an appropriately grown foetus on the 55th centile with an EFW of 3500 g, normal Dopplers and normal liquor.
verdicts.rationale: BMI/comorbidities/GBS/scan findings do not discriminate among the failed-forceps options. The discriminators (3 pulls with descent then no further, pathological CTG, theatre on standby) hook category 1 CS.

prunable_sentences: She had a previous uncomplicated spontaneous vaginal birth at 39+1 of a singleton with a normal third stage and no perineal trauma beyond a second-degree tear.
Her booking BMI was 26, the GBS swab at 36 weeks was negative and she has taken iron supplementation throughout the pregnancy with a current haemoglobin of 118 g/L.
verdicts.rationale: Prior singleton vaginal birth, BMI, GBS, iron supplementation, Hb do not discriminate among the twin-2 delivery options. The discriminators (transverse twin 2, intact membranes, reassuring CTG, experienced operator, theatre on standby) hook internal podalic version + breech extraction.

prunable_sentences: She is sexually active in a stable monogamous relationship of 6 years with no STI history, condom use is inconsistent, and last STI screen was 14 months ago and negative.
She works as a teacher and reports anxiety and disrupted sleep due to the pain.
verdicts.rationale: Sexual history/STI screening and occupational/psychosocial impact do not discriminate among the endometriosis-management options. The discriminators (subfertility, deep disease signs, endometrioma) are retained.

prunable_sentences: Her partner is a 35-year-old non-smoker with no children from previous relationships.
She is taking folic acid 0.5 mg daily.
Examination is unremarkable.
verdicts.rationale: Partner details, folic acid use, and unremarkable examination do not discriminate among the RPL workup options. The discriminators (3 consecutive losses, no SLE features, normal cavity, euploid POC, normal TSH/HbA1c) are retained.

prunable_sentences: There is no family history of infertility, premature menopause, or genetic disease on either side.
On her examination there are no abnormal findings; bimanual is normal with no nodularity.
verdicts.rationale: Family history of infertility/POI and normal examination do not discriminate among initial-investigation options. The discriminator is 13 months unprotected intercourse meeting infertility criteria.

prunable_sentences: She has no chronic disease.
verdicts.rationale: Chronic disease absence does not discriminate. The discriminators (severe functional impact, NSAID failure, family history of endometriosis, non-sexually-active) are retained.

prunable_sentences: She has fasted for 5 hours, has IV access in the right antecubital fossa, and morphine 5 mg IV has been given with partial relief.
Her parents have given consent for emergency anaesthesia and laparoscopy.
The gynaecology registrar and on-call anaesthetist have reviewed her and the operating theatre is available within 30 minutes.
verdicts.rationale: Fasting status, IV access details, analgesia history, consent, and team availability do not discriminate among the options - they are procedural anchors. The discriminators (whirlpool sign, absent venous Doppler, never-sexually-active rules out PID) are retained.

prunable_sentences: She is taking folic acid 0.5 mg daily.
Pelvic ultrasound today shows polycystic-appearing ovaries with no cysts or other pathology.
She has no other comorbidities.
She asks which agent is recommended as first-line ovulation induction in PCOS.
She has no other endocrine or metabolic comorbidity, no contraindication to letrozole or clomiphene, and she has had a pre-treatment FSH and oestradiol confirmed within normal range.
verdicts.rationale: Folic acid, current PCO ultrasound (already established), comorbidity absence, patient's question framing, and pre-treatment biochemistry (within normal) do not discriminate among letrozole/clomiphene/metformin/gonadotrophin/drilling options. Discriminators retained: PCOS with anovulatory infertility, normal partner semen, patent tubes, weight loss attempted, no contraindications.

prunable_sentences: She is highly motivated to preserve fertility, has a partner and is keen to avoid surgery if possible.
verdicts.rationale: This sentence duplicates 'She is keen to preserve future fertility' earlier. Removing it does not affect the option choice. Other features (apoplexy absence, gradual vision loss, prolactin 9800, macroadenoma with chiasm contact) are retained.

prunable_sentences: She has no chest pain or calf swelling but D-dimer is markedly elevated.
She has no abdominal peritonism on examination and a bedside ECG shows sinus tachycardia without ischaemic changes.
She has been counselled that severe OHSS is a recognised complication of high-response IVF.
verdicts.rationale: Absence of chest pain/calf swelling and elevated D-dimer support VTE risk (already addressed by OHSS context); abdominal peritonism absence and benign ECG do not discriminate among the management options. Counselling history is governance.

prunable_sentences: She has no history of menorrhagia, no current irregular bleeding and no contraindication to a copper intrauterine device.
verdicts.rationale: Menorrhagia/bleeding absence and lack of Cu-IUD contraindications are pre-emptive of the answer (option A) but do not discriminate among the options - all hormonal options are excluded by UKMEC 4 regardless of bleeding status. Note: Removing this sentence does not change difficulty.

prunable_sentences: She has no contraindication to an intrauterine system and has had a normal recent cervical screen and pelvic examination.
verdicts.rationale: IUS contraindication absence and cervical screen status do not discriminate - the discriminator is SLE + triple-positive aPL contraindication to combined contraception and DMPA UKMEC 3.

prunable_sentences: She has been advised that bariatric surgery, particularly Roux-en-Y gastric bypass, results in malabsorption of oral hormonal contraception and reduces the reliability of the combined oral contraceptive pill and the progestogen-only pill.

The bariatric service has also recommended deferring pregnancy for 12 to 24 months to allow weight stabilisation, to minimise rapid weight regain and to permit safe titration of micronutrient supplementation including thiamine, B12 and iron prior to conception.
verdicts.rationale: Both sentences ARE the explanation of why options B/C/E are wrong and are essentially telegraphing the answer. Without them the question still works for someone who knows post-bypass malabsorption affects orals and the 12-24 month deferral. These sentences are tutorial-text padding leaking the answer.

prunable_sentences: The team specifically asks how to apply the Kocher criteria today, whether MRI should be obtained before any intervention, whether oral antibiotics with outpatient orthopaedic review would be acceptable, and whether observation with serial CRP at 12 hours would be safer than immediate aspiration.
The on-call orthopaedic registrar is in theatre and can review within 30 minutes.
verdicts.rationale: The 'team asks' sentence enumerates the distractor options inline (option list summary), which is option-leaking padding. Registrar availability does not discriminate among options.

prunable_sentences: The ED registrar asks whether change to oral co-amoxiclav with 48-hour review would be safe, whether IV flucloxacillin alone provides adequate cover, whether immediate lumbar puncture is required given the fever, and whether MRI brain should precede antibiotics to exclude sigmoid sinus thrombosis before any intervention is commenced.
Imaging is available immediately.
verdicts.rationale: Registrar's enumerated questions are option-distractor leak (telegraphing options A-E). Imaging availability does not discriminate among options.

prunable_sentences: She was born at term by normal vaginal birth with an unremarkable antenatal course.
She passed the newborn hearing screen but the parents do not recall whether the red reflex was specifically examined on the newborn check or 6-week check.
The mother asks whether MRI or ophthalmology should come first.
verdicts.rationale: Birth history, newborn-hearing/red-reflex screening uncertainty, and mother's question are framing. None discriminate among the urgent-referral vs MRI vs optometry vs patching options.

prunable_sentences: The ED registrar specifically asks whether topical chloramphenicol could be trialled first, whether oral azithromycin for chlamydial conjunctivitis would be adequate, whether the appearance represents chemical conjunctivitis from birth, and whether topical aciclovir for neonatal HSV keratoconjunctivitis should be applied empirically.
The retrieval team is on the way.
verdicts.rationale: Registrar's enumerated questions telegraph options B/C/D/E and should not appear in the stem. Retrieval team status does not discriminate.

prunable_sentences: The ED consultant specifically asks whether 20 mL/kg fluid boluses are still recommended, whether vancomycin and meropenem are required, whether MRI should precede antibiotics, whether observation with paracetamol is acceptable, and whether ceftriaxone monotherapy with delayed fluid resuscitation pending arterial line insertion is reasonable.
The PICU bed coordinator has been notified and a bed is being made available.
verdicts.rationale: Consultant's enumerated questions telegraph options A-E and are option-leaking padding. PICU bed coordination is governance and does not discriminate.

prunable_sentences: His regular ICS is continued.
His parents are keen to leave once he is comfortable because they have other children at home.
The treating team specifically discusses the role of corticosteroids, the minimum versus extended observation period, and whether intensive-care admission is required given the two adrenaline doses.
verdicts.rationale: Continuation of ICS, parental keenness to leave, and team's enumerated questions do not discriminate among the disposition options. The discriminators (two adrenaline doses, asthma, transient collapse) are retained.

prunable_sentences: Previous attempts at empirical anti-allergic therapy with antihistamines and prednisolone produced no benefit.

The treating team specifically asks about the role of tryptase, of IgE testing, of imaging-led gastroenterology workup for the colicky abdominal pain, and whether tranexamic acid is appropriate as empirical long-term prophylaxis without further testing.
verdicts.rationale: Anti-allergic failure is duplicative with the earlier 'IM adrenaline / hydrocortisone / chlorphenamine with no apparent response' sentence. Team's enumerated questions telegraph options A/C/D/E.

prunable_sentences: He has no known allergies and is otherwise developmentally well.
The neurology team asks whether carbamazepine can be continued at half dose or switched within class.
The dermatology registrar specifically queries Stevens-Johnson syndrome and Kawasaki disease as alternative diagnoses.
The mother specifically wants to know whether the rash is contagious.
verdicts.rationale: PMH/developmental status, team queries enumerating options A/C/D/E, and mother's contagion question are all option-leaking or non-discriminating padding.

prunable_sentences: Her mother asks whether long-term oral corticosteroids are appropriate.
She has no asthma and no known allergies.

She specifically asks whether long-term oral corticosteroids, sedating antihistamines at night, prophylactic adrenaline, or ciclosporin should be the next step before considering biologic therapy with omalizumab.
School-attendance has become an issue and she is asking about a written sick certificate.
She has not previously trialled biologic therapy and asks about funding.
verdicts.rationale: Mother's question, asthma/allergies absence, patient's enumeration of options A/C/D/E, school certificate, and biologic funding are all telegraphing or non-discriminating.

prunable_sentences: He has had no recent travel, no contact with TB or invasive meningococcal disease and is unimmunised for varicella due to a planned MMR schedule slip.

The ED registrar specifically asks whether oral therapy with cefalexin and topical mupirocin would be sufficient for impetiginised varicella, and whether MRI of the thigh or intravenous immunoglobulin alone could substitute for early surgical exploration in this child.
verdicts.rationale: Travel/TB/meningococcal absence and varicella immunisation status do not discriminate among the necrotising-fasciitis options. Registrar's questions telegraph options C/D/E.

prunable_sentences: There is no Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander identification recorded.

The ED registrar specifically asks whether the trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole can be continued at half dose with oral prednisolone, whether the presentation could represent staphylococcal scalded skin syndrome or incomplete Kawasaki disease, and whether punch biopsy in ED should be performed before any therapy is commenced or transfer is initiated.
The family asks how long the recovery period typically takes after transfer.
verdicts.rationale: Identification status does not discriminate. Registrar's enumerated questions telegraph options B/C/D/E. Family's recovery question is irrelevant to next step choice.

prunable_sentences: She has no other serotonergic medications, no recreational MDMA, no St John's wort, no triptan use, and her partner confirms no other tablets at home.
The ED team is now choosing between supportive care, dantrolene, bromocriptine and benztropine.
verdicts.rationale: Absence of other serotonergic agents is already established by 'no recent illicit drug use' and the focus on sertraline+tramadol. The 'ED team choosing' sentence telegraphs options A/B/C/D.

prunable_sentences: She remains on clozapine titration with weekly FBC monitoring She has had no recent viral prodrome, no rash and no peripheral oedema.
The clozapine prescriber is contactable, and a cardiology bed is available if needed.
Echocardiogram and cardiac MRI can be arranged within hours through the on-call service She remains haemodynamically stable for now Myocarditis is the leading concern given timing.
verdicts.rationale: Duplicative information (no rash/oedema/viral prodrome already covered), governance details (prescriber contactable, beds available), and the diagnosis-leak sentence ('Myocarditis is the leading concern') telegraph the answer.

prunable_sentences: He takes no regular medications and weighs 70 kg He has no history of opioid use, no QT-prolonging medications, an ECG shows sinus rhythm with QTc 410 ms, and an unblocked IV cannula is in situ.

The medical team needs to choose a symptom-triggered regimen plus thiamine and electrolyte correction now He is requesting safer dosing than his prior alcohol detox He weighs 70 kg and is haemodynamically stable.
verdicts.rationale: Opioid use absence, QT/ECG details, IV cannula presence do not discriminate among the withdrawal regimen options. The 'team needs to choose' sentence telegraphs option B's components. Duplicate weight mention.

prunable_sentences: She is keen to start treatment today She has read about buprenorphine-naloxone and methadone and asks about safer first-day dosing for community induction given she cannot attend daily supervised dispensing She has heard from a friend that 16 mg sublingual buprenorphine at the first dose is the fastest way to suppress withdrawal and asks whether this is safe.
verdicts.rationale: Patient's enumeration of options (buprenorphine vs methadone, asking about 16mg) telegraphs options A/B/E. Already established that she cannot attend daily.

prunable_sentences: The local addiction medicine specialist is contactable and able to support either methadone dose adjustment or rotation to buprenorphine.

He has no syncope, no chest pain and no family history of long-QT syndrome, and his prior baseline ECG was unremarkable He is asking whether his methadone dose needs to be reduced today He is committed to engaging with treatment changes today.
verdicts.rationale: Specialist availability is governance. Family history of long-QT and patient questions about dose reduction telegraph option components. Baseline ECG (430ms) is already mentioned earlier.

prunable_sentences: He has no known allergies The ED senior is choosing between intramuscular sedation, oral benzodiazepine alone, ketamine, IV haloperidol or physical restraint without medication.
The on-call psychiatrist has been notified and is en route to assist with admission planning under the Mental Health Act if required after sedation The security team is also standing by.
verdicts.rationale: Allergy absence does not discriminate. The 'ED senior is choosing between' sentence telegraphs options A/B/C/D/E directly. Psychiatrist/security availability is governance.

prunable_sentences: Her partner reports she has previously responded to lithium and is keen to support any treatment, but he confirms she has not slept or eaten meaningfully for 48 hours.
The ED team is considering involuntary detention and admission under the Mental Health Act for assessment and stabilisation The duty psychiatrist is on the way.
verdicts.rationale: Partner's report duplicates earlier 'not slept in 36 hours' (now 48). The 'ED team is considering MHA detention' sentence telegraphs option A. Psychiatrist en route is governance.

prunable_sentences: The medical registrar is concerned about benzodiazepine accumulation given his cirrhosis, and is now considering escalation to a critical-care environment for safer titration and airway support if needed.

The pharmacist confirms lorazepam is available and is preferred in significant hepatic impairment because it has no active metabolites His electrolytes are pending and a magnesium level is being processed.
verdicts.rationale: Both sentences directly telegraph option A's components (lorazepam preferred, ICU/HDU escalation, electrolyte correction). They are tutorial-text leaking the answer.

prunable_sentences: Booking was uneventful.
She has had no episodes of vaginal bleeding, no abdominal pain, no rupture of membranes and no symptoms of pre-eclampsia.
Her booking BMI was 26 and her booking bloods including FBC, group and antibody screen and infection screen were unremarkable.
She has expressed a strong preference for any intervention that maximises the chance of two healthy survivors.
verdicts.rationale: Booking/PE/bleeding/AROM absence and booking bloods do not discriminate among TTTS management options. Patient preference does not change clinical answer. The discriminators (Quintero stage I features, gestation <26 weeks, foetal medicine availability) are retained.

prunable_sentences: The pregnancy was spontaneously conceived; she has had no episodes of vaginal bleeding, no rupture of membranes, no contractions and no symptoms of pre-eclampsia.
Booking BMI was 24, booking bloods were unremarkable and she has had no infections during the pregnancy.
The local maternity unit has a tertiary neonatal service on site with experienced obstetric and foetal-medicine input available daily.
She has had no abdominal pain and no recent infections of any kind.
verdicts.rationale: Spontaneous conception, absence of obstetric complications, booking bloods, BMI, unit-availability, and absence of pain/infection do not discriminate among the Gratacos sIUGR type-classification options. The discriminators (intermittent AEDF in smaller twin, normal liquor in both) are retained.

prunable_sentences: She has had no antepartum haemorrhage and her booking infection screen was unremarkable.
The neonatal team at the tertiary unit has confirmed cot availability; estimated transfer time by road is 90 minutes.
Indomethacin and prostaglandin synthetase inhibitors are stocked at the local unit, as are atosiban and nifedipine.
She has no contraindication to magnesium sulfate or to any tocolytic listed.
verdicts.rationale: Booking infection/APH, cot availability, drug stocking, and absence of tocolytic contraindications do not discriminate. The 'all options are available' framing implicitly telegraphs that no exclusion will eliminate options. The discriminators (chronic HTN on labetalol, aortic stenosis) hook atosiban over nifedipine via the hypotension interaction.

prunable_sentences: She has no other comorbidities.
She has no rupture of membranes, no bleeding, no fever and no offensive vaginal discharge.
Her observations are normal and a recent maternal infection screen is unremarkable.
The tertiary unit she is being transferred to has confirmed cot availability; estimated road transfer time is 75 minutes.
The team is making a decision about further antenatal corticosteroid dosing, aware that her first course finished more than 7 days ago and that the cervix is now actively dilating.
She has no other obstetric complication at present and her foetal observations remain reassuring.
verdicts.rationale: Multiple sentences are non-discriminating procedural framing or telegraph the answer's components ('first course > 7 days ago and cervix actively dilating' directly states option A's rationale). Discriminators retained: 31+5 gestation, prior complete course at 26+0, current threatened preterm labour.

prunable_sentences: She has no abdominal pain, no contractions, no foetal heart abnormality on ongoing CTG and no offensive vaginal discharge.
The amniotic fluid pool is clear and not bloodstained.
Maternal infection screen and FBC are unremarkable, and she has no allergies to penicillins or macrolides.

The plan is for inpatient expectant management to at least 34 weeks if she remains stable, with corticosteroids, magnesium sulfate for neuroprotection and antibiotic prophylaxis to prolong latency in keeping with current RANZCOG and Royal Women's PPROM guidance.
She has no contractions and no signs of clinical chorioamnionitis on serial review.
Erythromycin and amoxicillin-clavulanate are both stocked at the local maternity unit.
verdicts.rationale: Multiple sentences duplicate earlier content (no contractions, no fever already established), describe the plan inline (RANZCOG guidance summary leaks the answer), or are stocking/governance details. Discriminators retained: PPROM at 28+2, GBS negative, plan for latency antibiotics.

prunable_sentences: She is otherwise well and has no allergies.
The unit's neonatal services routinely accept babies from 35 weeks.
She has no contractions, no abdominal pain, no fever and the foetal heart is reassuring on continuous CTG.
Maternal observations and a recent FBC are unremarkable.
She has no penicillin allergy.
The neonatal unit on site routinely accepts babies from 35 weeks and there is no logistic barrier to immediate induction of labour.

The team is weighing immediate induction against short-term expectant management in the setting of PPROM beyond 36 weeks with confirmed group B streptococcal colonisation on her recent vaginal-rectal swab.
She has been counselled about both immediate induction and short-term expectant management options.
Induction of labour and intrapartum GBS prophylaxis can be commenced today.
verdicts.rationale: Multiple duplicative sentences (no contractions/fever twice, neonatal unit 35w twice), counselling/governance, and telegraphing of options A vs B. Discriminators retained: PPROM at 36+5, GBS positive, favourable cervix.

prunable_sentences: She has no chronic medical conditions and takes only folate and iodine.
Her last cervical screening is up to date and normal.
A detailed review of each loss documented painless cervical dilation as the dominant clinical feature, with rapid progression to delivery once membranes were exposed.
There was no antepartum haemorrhage, no abruption, no clinical chorioamnionitis and no documented infection at the time of any of the losses.
She has had no cervical surgery or LLETZ, no congenital uterine anomaly on previous imaging and no connective tissue disease.
She has been counselled about the recurrence risk after three painless mid-trimester losses.
verdicts.rationale: Folate/iodine, CST, duplicate restatement of painless dilation (already in opening), absence of APH/abruption/infection, prior LLETZ/anomaly absence, and counselling do not discriminate among cerclage/progesterone/pessary options for classic cervical insufficiency.

prunable_sentences: She has no history of stillbirth or previous large-for-gestational-age infant, and no obstetric or medical indication for early delivery.

Her ADIPS-aligned OGTT thresholds were borderline at booking but glycaemic control has been excellent on diet alone, with all fasting glucose values below 5.1 mmol/L and post-prandial values below 7.0 mmol/L.
The unit follows ADIPS and RANZCOG guidance which does not recommend routine early delivery before 39 weeks in well-controlled diet-managed GDM with normal foetal surveillance.
She has been counselled about the lack of evidence supporting routine early delivery in well-controlled diet-managed GDM.
She has been counselled about RANZCOG and ADIPS timing recommendations.
Her partner is supportive of expectant management.
verdicts.rationale: Multiple sentences directly state the answer's rationale (ADIPS guidance, no early delivery indication, counselling about evidence) - this is tutorial-text leaking the answer. Discriminators retained: diet-controlled GDM, 37+1 gestation, normal foetal surveillance.

prunable_sentences: There are no other localising features of infection.
She has no flank pain, no dysuria, no respiratory symptoms and no cellulitic or wound features on examination.
A FAST scan of the abdomen is unremarkable and her venous blood gas shows pH 7.36, lactate 2.1 mmol/L, base excess -2 and normal electrolytes.
The CTG over the past 30 minutes has been persistent foetal tachycardia at 170 to 180 bpm with reduced variability and no decelerations.
Her tertiary unit has a paediatric retrieval team on standby.
The team is balancing the risks of continued expectancy at this previable-leaning gestation against the strong clinical picture of clinical chorioamnionitis and early sepsis with foetal compromise.
verdicts.rationale: Absent localising features, FAST/VBG, duplicate CTG description, retrieval-team standby, and the diagnosis-leak 'strong clinical picture of clinical chorioamnionitis' sentence are padding. Discriminators retained: PPROM at 29+0, fever, foetal tachycardia, rising CRP, turbid AF.

prunable_sentences: She has had no vaginal bleeding and no rupture of membranes.
Her platelet count, INR, APTT and fibrinogen are within normal limits and a repeat coagulation profile in 6 hours is planned.

The MCA peak systolic velocity of 1.8 multiples of the median in the surviving twin exceeds the 1.5 MoM threshold widely used to suggest moderate to severe foetal anaemia, raising concern for an acute feto-foetal transfusion event at the time of co-twin demise in this monochorionic pregnancy.
The tertiary foetal medicine service two hours away offers ultrasound-guided intrauterine transfusion of the surviving twin with experienced operators available on a same-day basis.
She has been counselled on the risks of acute feto-foetal transfusion at co-twin demise.
verdicts.rationale: No bleeding/ROM does not discriminate. Coagulation profile is already covered by 'normal coagulation profile' earlier. The MCA interpretation sentence, transfusion-service availability, and counselling are tutorial-text leaking the answer. Discriminators retained: MC co-twin demise, MCA-PSV 1.8 MoM, surviving twin reactive.

prunable_sentences: She has no recent travel, no recent skin lesions and no flu-like systemic symptoms.
The Breast Centre has confirmed availability for ultrasound-guided aspiration the same afternoon if requested.
She has been counselled about office-based ultrasound-guided aspiration as the preferred first-line drainage option.
verdicts.rationale: Travel/skin lesions absence does not discriminate. Breast Centre availability and counselling sentence directly telegraph option B as 'preferred first-line drainage option' - tutorial-text leak.
